rules.label: Rules
rules.value:
🏁 PMR MX-5 League: Official Rules & Regulations 🏁
The primary focus of this league is developing strong, clean racecraft. Because MX-5 racing relies heavily on carrying momentum, smooth and fair driving will always yield the best results. Please review the rules and lobby settings below before heading out on track.

1. Overtaking & Defending
The Burden of Passing: The onus is always on the overtaking driver to execute a safe pass.

Defensive Moves: The car in front is entitled to defend their position, but they may not make erratic or multiple defensive moves to block an attacking car.

Earning the Corner: If an attacking car has a significant portion of their vehicle alongside (roughly level with the defending driver's shoulder) before the turn-in point, they have earned the right to the corner.

Leaving Space: When a car has earned the corner, the defending driver must leave "racing room" (at least one car's width) at the apex.

2. Steward Guidelines & Incidents
The Racing Line is Not Protected: Sticking blindly to the optimal racing line is not an excuse for causing a collision. If you force a driver off the track who has legitimately established an inside position, you will be penalized by the Stewards.

No Moving Under Braking: Altering your line in the braking zone to block a trailing car is strictly prohibited and will result in a penalty.

Contact & "Rubbing": Stewards understand that in close-quarters, momentum-based MX-5 racing, minor rubbing can happen. However, dangerous, careless, or intentional contact will not be tolerated under any circumstances.

Safe Re-entry: If you run off the track, you must yield to oncoming traffic. You may only rejoin the racing surface when it is absolutely safe to do so. Unsafe re-entries that cause collisions will be heavily penalized.

3. Official Lobby Settings
Damage: ON

In-Game Penalties: ON

Assists: AUTHENTIC

Track Temperature: AVERAGE (set to help preserve tyre life and promote closer racing).
